## File: gitlab/platform-gitops/.git/hooks/push-to-checkout.sample

#!/bin/sh

# An example hook script to update a checked-out tree on a git push.
#
# This hook is invoked by git-receive-pack(1) when it reacts to git
# push and updates reference(s) in its repository, and when the push
# tries to update the branch that is currently checked out and the
# receive.denyCurrentBranch configuration variable is set to
# updateInstead.
#
# By default, such a push is refused if the working tree and the index
# of the remote repository has any difference from the currently
# checked out commit; when both the working tree and the index match
# the current commit, they are updated to match the newly pushed tip
# of the branch. This hook is to be used to override the default
# behaviour; however the code below reimplements the default behaviour
# as a starting point for convenient modification.
#
# The hook receives the commit with which the tip of the current
# branch is going to be updated:
commit=$1

# It can exit with a non-zero status to refuse the push (when it does
# so, it must not modify the index or the working tree).
die () {
	echo >&2 "$*"
	exit 1
}

# Or it can make any necessary changes to the working tree and to the
# index to bring them to the desired state when the tip of the current
# branch is updated to the new commit, and exit with a zero status.
#
# For example, the hook can simply run git read-tree -u -m HEAD "$1"
# in order to emulate git fetch that is run in the reverse direction
# with git push, as the two-tree form of git read-tree -u -m is
# essentially the same as git switch or git checkout that switches
# branches while keeping the local changes in the working tree that do
# not interfere with the difference between the branches.

# The below is a more-or-less exact translation to shell of the C code
# for the default behaviour for git's push-to-checkout hook defined in
# the push_to_deploy() function in builtin/receive-pack.c.
#
# Note that the hook will be executed from the repository directory,
# not from the working tree, so if you want to perform operations on
# the working tree, you will have to adapt your code accordingly, e.g.
# by adding "cd .." or using relative paths.

if ! git update-index -q --ignore-submodules --refresh
then
	die "Up-to-date check failed"
fi

if ! git diff-files --quiet --ignore-submodules --
then
	die "Working directory has unstaged changes"
fi

# This is a rough translation of:
#
#   head_has_history() ? "HEAD" : EMPTY_TREE_SHA1_HEX
if git cat-file -e HEAD 2>/dev/null
then
	head=HEAD
else
	head=$(git hash-object -t tree --stdin </dev/null)
fi

if ! git diff-index --quiet --cached --ignore-submodules $head --
then
	die "Working directory has staged changes"
fi

if ! git read-tree -u -m "$commit"
then
	die "Could not update working tree to new HEAD"
fi

## File: gitlab/platform-gitops/.git/hooks/sendemail-validate.sample

#!/bin/sh

# An example hook script to validate a patch (and/or patch series) before
# sending it via email.
#
# The hook should exit with non-zero status after issuing an appropriate
# message if it wants to prevent the email(s) from being sent.
#
# To enable this hook, rename this file to "sendemail-validate".
#
# By default, it will only check that the patch(es) can be applied on top of
# the default upstream branch without conflicts in a secondary worktree. After
# validation (successful or not) of the last patch of a series, the worktree
# will be deleted.
#
# The following config variables can be set to change the default remote and
# remote ref that are used to apply the patches against:
#
#   sendemail.validateRemote (default: origin)
#   sendemail.validateRemoteRef (default: HEAD)
#
# Replace the TODO placeholders with appropriate checks according to your
# needs.

validate_cover_letter () {
	file="$1"
	# TODO: Replace with appropriate checks (e.g. spell checking).
	true
}

validate_patch () {
	file="$1"
	# Ensure that the patch applies without conflicts.
	git am -3 "$file" || return
	# TODO: Replace with appropriate checks for this patch
	# (e.g. checkpatch.pl).
	true
}

validate_series () {
	# TODO: Replace with appropriate checks for the whole series
	# (e.g. quick build, coding style checks, etc.).
	true
}

# main -------------------------------------------------------------------------

if test "$GIT_SENDEMAIL_FILE_COUNTER" = 1
then
	remote=$(git config --default origin --get sendemail.validateRemote) &&
	ref=$(git config --default HEAD --get sendemail.validateRemoteRef) &&
	worktree=$(mktemp --tmpdir -d sendemail-validate.XXXXXXX) &&
	git worktree add -fd --checkout "$worktree" "refs/remotes/$remote/$ref" &&
	git config --replace-all sendemail.validateWorktree "$worktree"
else
	worktree=$(git config --get sendemail.validateWorktree)
fi || {
	echo "sendemail-validate: error: failed to prepare worktree" >&2
	exit 1
}

unset GIT_DIR GIT_WORK_TREE
cd "$worktree" &&

if grep -q "^diff --git " "$1"
then
	validate_patch "$1"
else
	validate_cover_letter "$1"
fi &&

if test "$GIT_SENDEMAIL_FILE_COUNTER" = "$GIT_SENDEMAIL_FILE_TOTAL"
then
	git config --unset-all sendemail.validateWorktree &&
	trap 'git worktree remove -ff "$worktree"' EXIT &&
	validate_series
fi

## File: gitlab/platform-gitops/.git/hooks/update.sample

#!/bin/sh
#
# An example hook script to block unannotated tags from entering.
# Called by "git receive-pack" with arguments: refname sha1-old sha1-new
#
# To enable this hook, rename this file to "update".
#
# Config
# ------
# hooks.allowunannotated
#   This boolean sets whether unannotated tags will be allowed into the
#   repository.  By default they won't be.
# hooks.allowdeletetag
#   This boolean sets whether deleting tags will be allowed in the
#   repository.  By default they won't be.
# hooks.allowmodifytag
#   This boolean sets whether a tag may be modified after creation. By default
#   it won't be.
# hooks.allowdeletebranch
#   This boolean sets whether deleting branches will be allowed in the
#   repository.  By default they won't be.
# hooks.denycreatebranch
#   This boolean sets whether remotely creating branches will be denied
#   in the repository.  By default this is allowed.
#

# --- Command line
refname="$1"
oldrev="$2"
newrev="$3"

# --- Safety check
if [ -z "$GIT_DIR" ]; then
	echo "Don't run this script from the command line." >&2
	echo " (if you want, you could supply GIT_DIR then run" >&2
	echo "  $0 <ref> <oldrev> <newrev>)" >&2
	exit 1
fi

if [ -z "$refname" -o -z "$oldrev" -o -z "$newrev" ]; then
	echo "usage: $0 <ref> <oldrev> <newrev>" >&2
	exit 1
fi

# --- Config
allowunannotated=$(git config --type=bool hooks.allowunannotated)
allowdeletebranch=$(git config --type=bool hooks.allowdeletebranch)
denycreatebranch=$(git config --type=bool hooks.denycreatebranch)
allowdeletetag=$(git config --type=bool hooks.allowdeletetag)
allowmodifytag=$(git config --type=bool hooks.allowmodifytag)

# check for no description
projectdesc=$(sed -e '1q' "$GIT_DIR/description")
case "$projectdesc" in
"Unnamed repository"* | "")
	echo "*** Project description file hasn't been set" >&2
	exit 1
	;;
esac

# --- Check types
# if $newrev is 0000...0000, it's a commit to delete a ref.
zero=$(git hash-object --stdin </dev/null | tr '[0-9a-f]' '0')
if [ "$newrev" = "$zero" ]; then
	newrev_type=delete
else
	newrev_type=$(git cat-file -t $newrev)
fi

case "$refname","$newrev_type" in
	refs/tags/*,commit)
		# un-annotated tag
		short_refname=${refname##refs/tags/}
		if [ "$allowunannotated" != "true" ]; then
			echo "*** The un-annotated tag, $short_refname, is not allowed in this repository" >&2
			echo "*** Use 'git tag [ -a | -s ]' for tags you want to propagate." >&2
			exit 1
		fi
		;;
	refs/tags/*,delete)
		# delete tag
		if [ "$allowdeletetag" != "true" ]; then
			echo "*** Deleting a tag is not allowed in this repository" >&2
			exit 1
		fi
		;;
	refs/tags/*,tag)
		# annotated tag
		if [ "$allowmodifytag" != "true" ] && git rev-parse $refname > /dev/null 2>&1
		then
			echo "*** Tag '$refname' already exists." >&2
			echo "*** Modifying a tag is not allowed in this repository." >&2
			exit 1
		fi
		;;
	refs/heads/*,commit)
		# branch
		if [ "$oldrev" = "$zero" -a "$denycreatebranch" = "true" ]; then
			echo "*** Creating a branch is not allowed in this repository" >&2
			exit 1
		fi
		;;
	refs/heads/*,delete)
		# delete branch
		if [ "$allowdeletebranch" != "true" ]; then
			echo "*** Deleting a branch is not allowed in this repository" >&2
			exit 1
		fi
		;;
	refs/remotes/*,commit)
		# tracking branch
		;;
	refs/remotes/*,delete)
		# delete tracking branch
		if [ "$allowdeletebranch" != "true" ]; then
			echo "*** Deleting a tracking branch is not allowed in this repository" >&2
			exit 1
		fi
		;;
	*)
		# Anything else (is there anything else?)
		echo "*** Update hook: unknown type of update to ref $refname of type $newrev_type" >&2
		exit 1
		;;
esac

# --- Finished
exit 0

## File: gitlab/platform-gitops/.git/info/exclude

# git ls-files --others --exclude-from=.git/info/exclude
# Lines that start with '#' are comments.
# For a project mostly in C, the following would be a good set of
# exclude patterns (uncomment them if you want to use them):
# *.[oa]
# *~

## File: gitlab/platform-gitops/.gitignore

# Do not commit real credentials or generated kubeconfigs
*-secret.yaml
*.real.yaml
.env.local
*.pem
*.key
*.crt
kubeconfig*

## File: gitlab/platform-gitops/argocd/argocd-cr-avp-sidecar-patch-internal-image.example.yaml

# Production preferred pattern: use an internal image that already contains helm and argocd-vault-plugin.
spec:
  repo:
    volumes:
      - name: cmp-plugin
        configMap:
          name: cmp-plugin
      - name: cmp-tmp
        emptyDir: {}
      - name: avp-k8s-token
        projected:
          defaultMode: 420
          sources:
            - serviceAccountToken:
                path: token
                expirationSeconds: 3600
            - configMap:
                name: kube-root-ca.crt
                items:
                  - key: ca.crt
                    path: ca.crt
            - downwardAPI:
                items:
                  - path: namespace
                    fieldRef:
                      fieldPath: metadata.namespace
    sidecarContainers:
      - name: avp-helm
        image: registry.lac1.biz/gitops/avp-helm:1.18.0-helm3.14.4
        imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
        command:
          - /var/run/argocd/argocd-cmp-server
        env:
          - name: HELM_CACHE_HOME
            value: /tmp/helm/cache
          - name: HELM_CONFIG_HOME
            value: /tmp/helm/config
          - name: HELM_DATA_HOME
            value: /tmp/helm/data
          - name: AVP_CONFIG_TYPE
            value: env
          - name: ARGOCD_EXEC_TIMEOUT
            value: "180"
        securityContext:
          runAsNonRoot: true
          allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
          capabilities:
            drop: ["ALL"]
        volumeMounts:
          - name: var-files
            mountPath: /var/run/argocd
          - name: plugins
            mountPath: /home/argocd/cmp-server/plugins
          - name: cmp-tmp
            mountPath: /tmp
          - name: cmp-plugin
            mountPath: /home/argocd/cmp-server/config/plugin.yaml
            subPath: avp-helm.yaml
          - name: avp-k8s-token
            mountPath: /var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount
            readOnly: true

## File: gitlab/platform-gitops/argocd/argocd-cr-avp-sidecar-patch.yaml

spec:
  repo:
    volumes:
      - name: cmp-plugin
        configMap:
          name: cmp-plugin
      - name: custom-tools
        emptyDir: {}
      - name: cmp-tmp
        emptyDir: {}
      - name: avp-k8s-token
        projected:
          defaultMode: 420
          sources:
            - serviceAccountToken:
                path: token
                expirationSeconds: 3600
            - configMap:
                name: kube-root-ca.crt
                items:
                  - key: ca.crt
                    path: ca.crt
            - downwardAPI:
                items:
                  - path: namespace
                    fieldRef:
                      fieldPath: metadata.namespace
    initContainers:
      - name: download-tools
        image: registry.access.redhat.com/ubi9/ubi:latest
        imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
        env:
          - name: AVP_VERSION
            value: "1.18.0"
          - name: HELM_VERSION
            value: "v3.14.4"
        command: ["sh", "-c"]
        args:
          - |
            set -euo pipefail
            curl -fL "https://github.com/argoproj-labs/argocd-vault-plugin/releases/download/v${AVP_VERSION}/argocd-vault-plugin_${AVP_VERSION}_linux_amd64" -o /custom-tools/argocd-vault-plugin
            chmod +x /custom-tools/argocd-vault-plugin
            curl -fL "https://get.helm.sh/helm-${HELM_VERSION}-linux-amd64.tar.gz" -o /custom-tools/helm.tar.gz
            tar -xzf /custom-tools/helm.tar.gz -C /custom-tools
            mv /custom-tools/linux-amd64/helm /custom-tools/helm
            chmod +x /custom-tools/helm
            rm -rf /custom-tools/linux-amd64 /custom-tools/helm.tar.gz
            ls -l /custom-tools
        volumeMounts:
          - name: custom-tools
            mountPath: /custom-tools
    sidecarContainers:
      - name: avp-helm
        image: registry.access.redhat.com/ubi9/ubi:latest
        imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
        command:
          - /var/run/argocd/argocd-cmp-server
        env:
          - name: HELM_CACHE_HOME
            value: /tmp/helm/cache
          - name: HELM_CONFIG_HOME
            value: /tmp/helm/config
          - name: HELM_DATA_HOME
            value: /tmp/helm/data
          - name: ARGOCD_EXEC_TIMEOUT
            value: "180"
          # AVP Vault connection config — must live on the sidecar itself.
          # App-supplied plugin.env (AVP_SECRET) is renamed ARGOCD_ENV_* and
          # is invisible to AVP, so set it here. These are non-sensitive.
          - name: AVP_TYPE
            value: vault
          - name: AVP_AUTH_TYPE
            value: k8s
          - name: AVP_K8S_ROLE
            value: mas-gitops
          - name: VAULT_ADDR
            value: http://vault-active.vault.svc.cluster.local:8200
        securityContext:
          runAsNonRoot: true
          allowPrivilegeEscalation: false
          capabilities:
            drop:
              - ALL
        volumeMounts:
          - name: var-files
            mountPath: /var/run/argocd
          - name: plugins
            mountPath: /home/argocd/cmp-server/plugins
          - name: cmp-tmp
            mountPath: /tmp
          - name: cmp-plugin
            mountPath: /home/argocd/cmp-server/config/plugin.yaml
            subPath: avp-helm.yaml
          - name: custom-tools
            mountPath: /usr/local/bin/argocd-vault-plugin
            subPath: argocd-vault-plugin
          - name: custom-tools
            mountPath: /usr/local/bin/helm
            subPath: helm
          - name: avp-k8s-token
            mountPath: /var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount
            readOnly: true

## File: gitlab/platform-gitops/argocd/argocd-vault-plugin-credentials.aws-example.yaml

apiVersion: v1
kind: Secret
metadata:
  name: argocd-vault-plugin-credentials
  namespace: openshift-gitops
type: Opaque
stringData:
  AVP_TYPE: awssecretsmanager
  AWS_REGION: us-east-1
  AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID: CHANGE_ME
  AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY: CHANGE_ME

## File: gitlab/platform-gitops/argocd/argocd-vault-plugin-credentials.example.yaml

apiVersion: v1
kind: Secret
metadata:
  name: argocd-vault-plugin-credentials
  namespace: openshift-gitops
type: Opaque
stringData:
  AVP_TYPE: vault
  AVP_AUTH_TYPE: k8s
  AVP_K8S_ROLE: mas-gitops
  VAULT_ADDR: http://vault-active.vault.svc.cluster.local:8200

## File: gitlab/platform-gitops/argocd/cmp-plugin-configmap.yaml

apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: cmp-plugin
  namespace: openshift-gitops
  annotations:
    argocd.argoproj.io/sync-wave: "20"
data:
  avp-helm.yaml: |
    apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1
    kind: ConfigManagementPlugin
    metadata:
      name: argocd-vault-plugin-helm
    spec:
      init:
        command: ["sh", "-c"]
        args: ["helm dependency build || true"]
      generate:
        command: ["sh", "-c"]
        args:
          - 'helm template "$ARGOCD_APP_NAME" --namespace "$ARGOCD_APP_NAMESPACE" --include-crds -f <(echo "$ARGOCD_ENV_HELM_VALUES") . | argocd-vault-plugin generate -'
      discover:
        find:
          command: ["sh", "-c"]
          args: ["find . -name Chart.yaml"]

## File: gitlab/platform-gitops/bootstrap/01-argocd-cluster-admin-rbac.yaml

# Optional but common for platform/management ArgoCD.
# Requires platform/security approval. Apply once as cluster-admin.
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRoleBinding
metadata:
  name: openshift-gitops-application-controller-cluster-admin
subjects:
  - kind: ServiceAccount
    name: openshift-gitops-argocd-application-controller
    namespace: openshift-gitops
roleRef:
  kind: ClusterRole
  name: cluster-admin
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io

## File: gitlab/platform-gitops/bootstrap/02-platform-app-of-apps.yaml

# Apply ONCE on the MANAGEMENT cluster after repo credentials and GitLab CA trust are in place:
#   oc apply -f bootstrap.yaml
apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1
kind: Application
metadata:
  name: platform-app-of-apps
  namespace: openshift-gitops
spec:
  # Keep bootstrap in default to avoid chicken-and-egg. The chart creates AppProject "mas".
  project: default
  sources:
    - repoURL: https://gitlab.lac1.biz/gitops/platform-gitops.git
      targetRevision: main
      path: charts/app-of-apps
      helm:
        valueFiles:
          - $values/values/values-management.yaml
    - repoURL: https://gitlab.lac1.biz/gitops/platform-gitops.git
      targetRevision: main
      ref: values
  destination:
    server: https://kubernetes.default.svc
    namespace: openshift-gitops
  syncPolicy:
    automated:
      prune: false
      selfHeal: true
    syncOptions:
      - CreateNamespace=false

## File: gitlab/platform-gitops/charts/app-of-apps/Chart.yaml

apiVersion: v2
name: app-of-apps
description: App-of-apps for IBM MAS GitOps prerequisites
version: 0.1.0
type: application

## File: gitlab/platform-gitops/charts/app-of-apps/templates/00-app-project.yaml

apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1
kind: AppProject
metadata:
  name: mas
  namespace: {{ .Values.argoNamespace }}
  annotations:
    argocd.argoproj.io/sync-wave: "0"
spec:
  description: IBM MAS GitOps management project
  sourceRepos:
    - {{ .Values.source.repo_url | quote }}
    - {{ .Values.generator.repo_url | quote }}
    - https://helm.releases.hashicorp.com
    - https://gitlab.lac1.biz/gitops/platform-gitops.git
  destinations:
    - server: '*'
      namespace: '*'
  clusterResourceWhitelist:
    - group: '*'
      kind: '*'
  namespaceResourceWhitelist:
    - group: '*'
      kind: '*'

## File: gitlab/platform-gitops/charts/app-of-apps/templates/10-hashicorp-vault-server.yaml

{{- if .Values.vault.enabled }}
apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1
kind: Application
metadata:
  name: hashicorp-vault-server
  namespace: {{ .Values.argoNamespace }}
  annotations:
    argocd.argoproj.io/sync-wave: "10"
spec:
  project: mas
  source:
    repoURL: https://helm.releases.hashicorp.com
    chart: vault
    targetRevision: {{ .Values.vault.chartVersion | quote }}
    helm:
      releaseName: vault
      values: |
        global:
          openshift: true
          enabled: true
        injector:
          enabled: false
        server:
          enabled: true
          image:
            repository: {{ .Values.vault.imageRepository }}
            tag: {{ .Values.vault.imageTag | quote }}
          route:
            enabled: true
            host: {{ .Values.vault.host }}
            tls:
              termination: edge
          dataStorage:
            enabled: true
            size: {{ .Values.vault.dataSize }}
            storageClass: {{ .Values.vault.storageClass }}
            accessMode: ReadWriteOnce
          auditStorage:
            enabled: true
            size: {{ .Values.vault.auditSize }}
            storageClass: {{ .Values.vault.storageClass }}
            accessMode: ReadWriteOnce
          ha:
            enabled: true
            replicas: {{ .Values.vault.replicas }}
            raft:
              enabled: true
              setNodeId: true
              config: |
                ui = true
                listener "tcp" {
                  address = "[::]:8200"
                  cluster_address = "[::]:8201"
                  tls_disable = 1
                }
                storage "raft" {
                  path = "/vault/data"
                  retry_join { leader_api_addr = "http://vault-0.vault-internal:8200" }
                  retry_join { leader_api_addr = "http://vault-1.vault-internal:8200" }
                  retry_join { leader_api_addr = "http://vault-2.vault-internal:8200" }
                }
                service_registration "kubernetes" {}
          readinessProbe:
            enabled: true
            path: "/v1/sys/health?standbyok=true&sealedcode=204&uninitcode=204"
          livenessProbe:
            enabled: false
        ui:
          enabled: true
          serviceType: ClusterIP
  destination:
    server: https://kubernetes.default.svc
    namespace: vault
  syncPolicy:
    automated:
      prune: false
      selfHeal: true
    syncOptions:
      - CreateNamespace=true
{{- end }}

## File: gitlab/platform-gitops/charts/app-of-apps/templates/20-argocd-vault-plugin.yaml

apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: cmp-plugin
  namespace: {{ .Values.argoNamespace }}
  annotations:
    argocd.argoproj.io/sync-wave: "20"
data:
  avp-helm.yaml: |
    apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1
    kind: ConfigManagementPlugin
    metadata:
      name: argocd-vault-plugin-helm
    spec:
      init:
        command: ["sh", "-c"]
        args: ["helm dependency build || true"]
      generate:
        command: ["sh", "-c"]
        args:
          - 'helm template "$ARGOCD_APP_NAME" --namespace "$ARGOCD_APP_NAMESPACE" --include-crds -f <(echo "$ARGOCD_ENV_HELM_VALUES") . | argocd-vault-plugin generate -'
      discover:
        find:
          command: ["sh", "-c"]
          args: ["find . -name Chart.yaml"]
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Secret
metadata:
  name: argocd-vault-plugin-credentials
  namespace: {{ .Values.argoNamespace }}
  annotations:
    argocd.argoproj.io/sync-wave: "20"
type: Opaque
stringData:
  AVP_TYPE: vault
  AVP_AUTH_TYPE: k8s
  AVP_K8S_ROLE: {{ .Values.avp.role | quote }}
  VAULT_ADDR: {{ include "appofapps.vaultAddr" . | quote }}
---
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRoleBinding
metadata:
  name: argocd-repo-server-tokenreview
  annotations:
    argocd.argoproj.io/sync-wave: "20"
roleRef:
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
  kind: ClusterRole
  name: system:auth-delegator
subjects:
  - kind: ServiceAccount
    name: {{ .Values.repoServerServiceAccount | quote }}
    namespace: {{ .Values.argoNamespace | quote }}

## File: gitlab/platform-gitops/charts/app-of-apps/templates/30-ibm-mas-account-root.yaml

apiVersion: argoproj.io/v1alpha1
kind: Application
metadata:
  name: ibm-mas-account-root
  namespace: {{ .Values.argoNamespace }}
  annotations:
    argocd.argoproj.io/sync-wave: "30"
spec:
  project: mas
  destination:
    namespace: {{ .Values.argoNamespace }}
    server: https://kubernetes.default.svc
  source:
    repoURL: {{ .Values.source.repo_url | quote }}
    targetRevision: {{ .Values.source.revision | quote }}
    path: root-applications/ibm-mas-account-root
    helm:
      values: |
        avp:
          name: argocd-vault-plugin-helm
          secret: argocd-vault-plugin-credentials
          values_varname: HELM_VALUES
        account:
          id: {{ .Values.account.id }}
        source:
          repo_url: {{ .Values.source.repo_url }}
          revision: {{ .Values.source.revision }}
        generator:
          repo_url: {{ .Values.generator.repo_url }}
          revision: {{ .Values.generator.revision }}
        argo:
          namespace: {{ .Values.argoNamespace }}
          projects:
            rootapps: mas
            apps: mas
        cluster_admin_role: {{ .Values.accountRoot.clusterAdminRole }}
        application_admin_role: {{ .Values.accountRoot.applicationAdminRole }}
        auto_delete: false
{{- if .Values.accountRoot.autoSync }}
  syncPolicy:
    automated:
      prune: false
      selfHeal: true
{{- end }}

## File: gitlab/platform-gitops/charts/app-of-apps/templates/_helpers.tpl

{{- define "appofapps.vaultAddr" -}}
{{- if .Values.avp.vaultAddr -}}{{ .Values.avp.vaultAddr }}{{- else -}}https://{{ .Values.vault.host }}{{- end -}}
{{- end -}}

## File: gitlab/platform-gitops/charts/app-of-apps/values.yaml

account:
  id: mas

argoNamespace: openshift-gitops

# IMPORTANT: Use the actual repo-server service account name.
# In your current cluster this was observed as "default".
repoServerServiceAccount: default

source:
  repo_url: https://gitlab.lac1.biz/gitops/ibm-mas-gitops.git
  revision: "8.0.0"

generator:
  repo_url: https://gitlab.lac1.biz/gitops/mas-gitops-config.git
  revision: main

vault:
  enabled: true
  host: CHANGE_ME_VAULT_HOST
  chartVersion: "0.32.0"
  imageRepository: registry.connect.redhat.com/hashicorp/vault
  imageTag: "1.21.2-ubi"
  storageClass: isilon
  dataSize: 20Gi
  auditSize: 10Gi
  replicas: 3

avp:
  role: mas-gitops
  # Use in-cluster service URL to avoid external route TLS issues.
  vaultAddr: http://vault-active.vault.svc.cluster.local:8200

accountRoot:
  autoSync: false
  clusterAdminRole: true
  applicationAdminRole: true

## File: gitlab/platform-gitops/docs/DEPLOYMENT-STEPS.md

# Deployment steps

## Phase 1: Git trust and repo credentials

```bash
oc apply -f bootstrap/00-gitlab-ca-configmap.yaml
oc apply -f bootstrap/01-argocd-cluster-admin-rbac.yaml
oc apply -f <real-repo-creds-secret.yaml>
oc rollout restart deploy/openshift-gitops-repo-server -n openshift-gitops
```

## Phase 2: bootstrap app-of-apps app

```bash
oc apply -f bootstrap.yaml
```

Sync `platform-app-of-apps`, then sync `hashicorp-vault-server`.

## Phase 3: Vault init/unseal and auth

```bash
oc exec -n vault vault-0 -- vault operator init
export VAULT_TOKEN='<root-or-admin-token>'
./vault-auth/setup-vault-auth.sh
```

## Phase 4: AVP sidecar

```bash
oc patch argocd openshift-gitops -n openshift-gitops --type merge --patch-file argocd/argocd-cr-avp-sidecar-patch.yaml
oc rollout restart deploy/openshift-gitops-repo-server -n openshift-gitops
oc rollout status deploy/openshift-gitops-repo-server -n openshift-gitops
./vault-auth/test-avp.sh
```

## Phase 5: MAS

Do not sync `ibm-mas-account-root` until target clusters are registered, MAS config repo is pushed, real MAS secrets are loaded, and AVP test passes.

    argocd.argoproj.io/secret-type: repo-creds

kind: Secret
metadata:
  name: gitlab-gitops-group-repo-creds
  namespace: openshift-gitops
  labels:
    argocd.argoproj.io/secret-type: repo-creds
type: Opaque

  username: CHANGE_ME_DEPLOY_TOKEN_USERNAME
  password: CHANGE_ME_DEPLOY_TOKEN_VALUE

## File: gitlab/platform-gitops/scripts/sync-mongo-ca.sh

#!/usr/bin/env bash
#
# Sync the live mas-mongo-ce CA into Vault, and trigger SLS to pick it up.
#
# Run this after any Mongo redeploy / CA rotation. It is idempotent: if the
# CA in Vault already matches the cluster, it does nothing.
#
# Why a refresh at the end: AVP only resolves <path:...> placeholders when
# ArgoCD regenerates the app's manifests. Updating Vault alone will NOT reach
# a running SLS until the SLS Application is hard-refreshed.
#
# Usage:
#   export VAULT_TOKEN='<admin-token-with-write-on-the-path>'
#   name: sls.{{ .Values.cluster.id }}.{{ .Values.instance.id }}
#   SLS_APP=sls.drroc4.drgitopsapp ./scripts/sync-mongo-ca.sh
#
set -euo pipefail

# ---- what / where ----------------------------------------------------------
MONGO_NS="${MONGO_NS:-mongoce}"
MONGO_CR="${MONGO_CR:-mas-mongo-ce}"
VAULT_NAMESPACE="${VAULT_NAMESPACE:-vault}"
VAULT_POD="${VAULT_POD:-vault-0}"
VAULT_ADDR_IN_POD="${VAULT_ADDR_IN_POD:-http://127.0.0.1:8200}"
VAULT_PATH="${VAULT_PATH:-secret/mas/drroc4/drgitopsapp/sls-mongo}"
VAULT_KEY="${VAULT_KEY:-ca.crt}"
ARGO_NAMESPACE="${ARGO_NAMESPACE:-openshift-gitops}"
SLS_APP="${SLS_APP:-}"   # optional: ArgoCD Application name to hard-refresh

if [[ -z "${VAULT_TOKEN:-}" ]]; then echo "ERROR: export VAULT_TOKEN first" >&2; exit 1; fi
command -v oc >/dev/null || { echo "ERROR: oc not found" >&2; exit 1; }

vx() { oc exec -n "$VAULT_NAMESPACE" "$VAULT_POD" -- sh -c \
  "export VAULT_ADDR=$VAULT_ADDR_IN_POD; export VAULT_TOKEN='$VAULT_TOKEN'; $*"; }

TMP="$(mktemp -d)"; trap 'rm -rf "$TMP"' EXIT

# ---- 1) pull the live CA off the Mongo CR ----------------------------------
CACM="$(oc get mongodbcommunity "$MONGO_CR" -n "$MONGO_NS" \
  -o jsonpath='{.spec.security.tls.caConfigMapRef.name}' 2>/dev/null || true)"
if [[ -n "$CACM" ]]; then
  oc get configmap "$CACM" -n "$MONGO_NS" -o jsonpath='{.data.ca\.crt}' > "$TMP/ca.crt"
else
  CASEC="$(oc get mongodbcommunity "$MONGO_CR" -n "$MONGO_NS" \
    -o jsonpath='{.spec.security.tls.certificateKeySecretRef.name}')"
  oc get secret "$CASEC" -n "$MONGO_NS" -o jsonpath='{.data.ca\.crt}' | base64 -d > "$TMP/ca.crt"
fi
[[ -s "$TMP/ca.crt" ]] || { echo "ERROR: no CA found on $MONGO_NS/$MONGO_CR" >&2; exit 1; }

# ---- 2) escape newlines (matches the inline AVP placeholder usage) ----------
sed ':a;N;$!ba;s/\n/\\n/g' "$TMP/ca.crt" > "$TMP/ca.escaped"
NEW="$(cat "$TMP/ca.escaped")"

# ---- 3) compare with what's already in Vault; no-op if identical -----------
CUR="$(vx "vault kv get -field=$VAULT_KEY $VAULT_PATH" 2>/dev/null || true)"
if [[ "$NEW" == "$CUR" ]]; then
  echo "CA already current in Vault ($VAULT_PATH#$VAULT_KEY) — nothing to do."
  exit 0
fi

# ---- 4) write the new CA ---------------------------------------------------
oc cp "$TMP/ca.escaped" "$VAULT_NAMESPACE/$VAULT_POD:/tmp/mongo-ca.escaped"
vx "vault kv put $VAULT_PATH $VAULT_KEY=@/tmp/mongo-ca.escaped; rm -f /tmp/mongo-ca.escaped"
echo "CA updated in Vault: $VAULT_PATH#$VAULT_KEY"

# ---- 5) make SLS re-resolve it ---------------------------------------------
if [[ -n "$SLS_APP" ]]; then
  oc annotate application "$SLS_APP" -n "$ARGO_NAMESPACE" \
    argocd.argoproj.io/refresh=hard --overwrite
  echo "Triggered hard refresh of $SLS_APP — AVP will re-render with the new CA."
else
  echo "NOTE: set SLS_APP=<sls application name> to auto-refresh, or hard-refresh"
  echo "      the SLS app manually so AVP re-resolves the placeholder."
fi

## File: gitlab/platform-gitops/scripts/validate-files.sh

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
for f in $(find . -name '*.yaml' -o -name '*.yml'); do
  python3 -c "import yaml; list(yaml.safe_load_all(open('$f'))); print('ok $f')"
done

## File: gitlab/platform-gitops/scripts/verify-platform.sh

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
oc get application -n openshift-gitops || true
oc get pods -n vault || true
oc get route -n vault || true
POD="$(oc get pod -n openshift-gitops | awk '/repo-server/ {print $1; exit}')"
echo "repo-server pod: ${POD}"
oc get pod "${POD}" -n openshift-gitops -o jsonpath='{.spec.containers[*].name}{"\n"}'
oc exec -n openshift-gitops "${POD}" -c avp-helm -- argocd-vault-plugin version || true
oc exec -n openshift-gitops "${POD}" -c avp-helm -- helm version || true

## File: gitlab/platform-gitops/setup.md

# Platform GitOps Setup Guide for IBM MAS GitOps

This guide explains the end-to-end deployment order for the `platform-gitops` repository.

The goal of this repo is to prepare the management OpenShift GitOps / ArgoCD environment for IBM MAS GitOps by installing and configuring GitLab trust, ArgoCD permissions, HashiCorp Vault, Vault auth/policy, ArgoCD Vault Plugin, and the IBM MAS Account Root Application.

> Important: Real secrets, Vault root tokens, Vault unseal keys, GitLab tokens, MAS passwords, license files, database passwords, and crypto keys must never be committed to Git.

## 1. Repository layout

```text
platform-gitops/
├── bootstrap/
│   ├── 00-gitlab-ca-configmap.yaml
│   ├── 01-argocd-cluster-admin-rbac.yaml
│   └── 02-platform-app-of-apps.yaml
├── repo-creds/
│   └── gitlab-group-repo-creds.example.yaml
├── charts/app-of-apps/
├── argocd/
├── values/
├── vault-auth/
├── scripts/
├── docs/
└── README.md
```

## 2. Deployment order

Use this exact order:

```text
1. Configure GitLab CA trust
2. Configure ArgoCD platform RBAC
3. Configure GitLab repo credentials
4. Update values/values-management.yaml
5. Deploy platform-app-of-apps
6. Sync Vault server
7. Initialize, join, and unseal Vault
8. Configure Vault KV, policy, and Kubernetes auth
9. Configure AVP credentials
10. Patch ArgoCD repo-server with AVP sidecar
11. Test AVP with a dummy Vault secret
12. Load real MAS secrets into Vault
13. Prepare/commit mas-gitops-config repo
14. Register target cluster in management ArgoCD
15. Sync IBM MAS Account Root
16. Deploy MAS Core first
17. Deploy Manage only after DB/JDBC is confirmed
```

## 3. Phase 1: Configure GitLab CA trust

ArgoCD repo-server must trust the internal GitLab TLS certificate.

### 3.1 Generate GitLab CA bundle

Run this from a machine that can reach GitLab:

```bash
echo | openssl s_client -showcerts \
  -connect gitlab.lacl.biz:443 \
  -servername gitlab.lacl.biz 2>/dev/null \
| awk '/-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----/{flag=1} flag{print} /-----END CERTIFICATE-----/{flag=0}' \
> gitlab-lacl-chain.pem
```

Verify:

```bash
grep -n "BEGIN CERTIFICATE" gitlab-lacl-chain.pem
```

Split the certificates:

```bash
csplit -f cert- -b "%02d.pem" gitlab-lacl-chain.pem '/-----BEGIN CERTIFICATE-----/' '{*}'
```

Inspect them:

```bash
for f in cert-*.pem; do
  echo "==== $f ===="
  openssl x509 -in "$f" -noout -subject -issuer
done
```

For our environment:

```text
cert-01.pem = GitLab server cert
cert-02.pem = Spire-SubCA
cert-03.pem = Spire-RootCA
```

Create the CA bundle:

```bash
cat cert-02.pem cert-03.pem > gitlab-lacl-ca-bundle.pem
```

Update the GitOps file:

```bash
cat > bootstrap/00-gitlab-ca-configmap.yaml <<EOF
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: argocd-tls-certs-cm
  namespace: openshift-gitops
data:
  gitlab.lacl.biz: |
$(sed 's/^/    /' gitlab-lacl-ca-bundle.pem)
EOF
```

Apply and restart repo-server:

```bash
oc apply -f bootstrap/00-gitlab-ca-configmap.yaml
oc rollout restart deploy/openshift-gitops-repo-server -n openshift-gitops
oc rollout status deploy/openshift-gitops-repo-server -n openshift-gitops
```

Verify:

```bash
oc exec -n openshift-gitops deploy/openshift-gitops-repo-server -- ls -l /app/config/tls
oc exec -n openshift-gitops deploy/openshift-gitops-repo-server -- grep -n "BEGIN CERTIFICATE" /app/config/tls/gitlab.lacl.biz
```

Expected: two certificates in `/app/config/tls/gitlab.lacl.biz`.

## 4. Phase 2: Configure ArgoCD platform RBAC

Apply:

```bash
oc apply -f bootstrap/01-argocd-cluster-admin-rbac.yaml
```

Verify:

```bash
oc auth can-i '*' '*' \
  --as=system:serviceaccount:openshift-gitops:openshift-gitops-argocd-application-controller
```

Expected:

```text
yes
```

This allows ArgoCD to manage platform resources such as Vault, routes, services, statefulsets, and cluster-level resources. If security does not allow cluster-admin, use namespace-scoped RoleBindings instead, but then you must grant each namespace separately.

## 5. Phase 3: Configure GitLab repo credentials

Copy the example:

```bash
cp repo-creds/gitlab-group-repo-creds.example.yaml /tmp/gitlab-group-repo-creds.yaml
vi /tmp/gitlab-group-repo-creds.yaml
```

Update:

```yaml
username: <GitLab group deploy token username>
password: <GitLab group deploy token value>
```

Apply:

```bash
oc apply -f /tmp/gitlab-group-repo-creds.yaml
```

Verify:

```bash
oc get secret gitlab-gitops-group-repo-creds -n openshift-gitops
```

Test repo access:

```bash
oc exec -n openshift-gitops deploy/openshift-gitops-repo-server -- \
  sh -c 'GIT_SSL_CAINFO=/app/config/tls/gitlab.lacl.biz git ls-remote https://gitlab.lacl.biz/gitops/platform-gitops.git'
```

If you see refs, Git access is working. If you see authentication errors, check the token username, token value, and `read_repository` scope.

## 6. Phase 4: Update management values

Edit:

```bash
vi values/values-management.yaml
```

Confirm/update:

```yaml
account:
  id: mas

source:
  repo_url: https://gitlab.lacl.biz/gitops/ibm-mas-gitops.git
  revision: "8.0.0"

generator:
  repo_url: https://gitlab.lacl.biz/gitops/mas-gitops-config.git
  revision: main

vault:
  enabled: true
  host: vault.apps.drroc4.lac1.biz

avp:
  role: mas-gitops
```

## 7. Phase 5: Deploy platform app-of-apps

Apply:

```bash
oc apply -f bootstrap/02-platform-app-of-apps.yaml
```

Verify:

```bash
oc get applications -n openshift-gitops
```

Expected:

```text
platform-app-of-apps
```

This parent app creates child apps from `charts/app-of-apps/templates/`.

Do not sync IBM MAS Account Root yet.

## 8. Phase 6: Sync Vault server

Sync only Vault first:

```bash
argocd app sync hashicorp-vault-server
```

Verify:

```bash
oc get pods -n vault
oc get svc -n vault
oc get route -n vault
```

Expected:

```text
vault-0
vault-1
vault-2
```

## 9. Phase 7: Initialize, join, and unseal Vault

This phase stays manual because it creates root token and unseal keys.

Initialize only `vault-0`:

```bash
oc exec -n vault vault-0 -- vault operator init
```

Save securely:

- Unseal keys
- Initial root token

Do not store them in Git.

Unseal `vault-0`:

```bash
oc exec -n vault vault-0 -- vault operator unseal '<KEY1>'
oc exec -n vault vault-0 -- vault operator unseal '<KEY2>'
oc exec -n vault vault-0 -- vault operator unseal '<KEY3>'
```

Verify:

```bash
oc exec -n vault vault-0 -- vault status
```

Expected:

```text
Initialized: true
Sealed: false
```

Join `vault-1` and `vault-2`:

```bash
oc exec -n vault vault-1 -- sh -c 'VAULT_ADDR=http://127.0.0.1:8200 vault operator raft join http://vault-0.vault-internal:8200'
oc exec -n vault vault-2 -- sh -c 'VAULT_ADDR=http://127.0.0.1:8200 vault operator raft join http://vault-0.vault-internal:8200'
```

Unseal `vault-1` and `vault-2`:

```bash
oc exec -n vault vault-1 -- vault operator unseal '<KEY1>'
oc exec -n vault vault-1 -- vault operator unseal '<KEY2>'
oc exec -n vault vault-1 -- vault operator unseal '<KEY3>'

oc exec -n vault vault-2 -- vault operator unseal '<KEY1>'
oc exec -n vault vault-2 -- vault operator unseal '<KEY2>'
oc exec -n vault vault-2 -- vault operator unseal '<KEY3>'
```

Verify all pods:

```bash
oc exec -n vault vault-0 -- vault status
oc exec -n vault vault-1 -- vault status
oc exec -n vault vault-2 -- vault status
oc exec -n vault vault-0 -- vault operator raft list-peers
```

Expected:

```text
Initialized: true
Sealed: false
vault-0
vault-1
vault-2
```

## 10. Phase 8: Configure Vault auth and policy for AVP

Run:

```bash
chmod +x vault-auth/setup-vault-auth.sh
./vault-auth/setup-vault-auth.sh
```

This configures:

- `secret/` KV v2 engine
- `mas-gitops` Vault policy
- Kubernetes auth backend
- `mas-gitops` Vault role

The Vault policy allows AVP to read only:

```text
secret/data/mas/*
secret/metadata/mas/*
```

Verify:

```bash
oc exec -n vault vault-0 -- sh -c '
export VAULT_ADDR=http://127.0.0.1:8200
vault policy read mas-gitops
vault read auth/kubernetes/role/mas-gitops
'
```

## 11. Phase 9: Configure AVP credentials

Copy and update:

```bash
cp argocd/argocd-vault-plugin-credentials.example.yaml /tmp/argocd-vault-plugin-credentials.yaml
vi /tmp/argocd-vault-plugin-credentials.yaml
```

Expected values:

```yaml
AVP_TYPE: vault
AVP_AUTH_TYPE: k8s
AVP_K8S_ROLE: mas-gitops
VAULT_ADDR: http://vault-active.vault.svc.cluster.local:8200
```

Apply:

```bash
oc apply -f /tmp/argocd-vault-plugin-credentials.yaml
```

Verify:

```bash
oc get secret argocd-vault-plugin-credentials -n openshift-gitops
```

## 12. Phase 10: Configure AVP sidecar in repo-server

Apply the working patch:

```bash
oc patch argocd openshift-gitops \
  -n openshift-gitops \
  --type merge \
  --patch-file argocd/argocd-cr-avp-sidecar-patch.yaml
```

Restart repo-server:

```bash
oc rollout restart deploy/openshift-gitops-repo-server -n openshift-gitops
oc rollout status deploy/openshift-gitops-repo-server -n openshift-gitops
```

Verify containers:

```bash
POD=$(oc get pod -n openshift-gitops | awk '/repo-server/ {print $1; exit}')

oc get pod "$POD" -n openshift-gitops \
  -o jsonpath='{.spec.containers[*].name}{"\n"}'
```

Expected:

```text
argocd-repo-server avp-helm
```

Verify tools:

```bash
oc exec -n openshift-gitops "$POD" -c avp-helm -- argocd-vault-plugin version
oc exec -n openshift-gitops "$POD" -c avp-helm -- helm version
```

Expected:

```text
argocd-vault-plugin v1.18.0
helm v3.14.4
```

Verify token mount:

```bash
oc exec -n openshift-gitops "$POD" -c avp-helm -- \
  ls -l /var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount
```

Expected:

```text
ca.crt
namespace
token
```

## 13. Phase 11: Test AVP manually

Run:

```bash
chmod +x vault-auth/test-avp.sh
./vault-auth/test-avp.sh
```

Expected output:

```yaml
apiVersion: v1
data:
  message: hello-from-vault
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: avp-test
  namespace: openshift-gitops
```

This confirms:

- Vault is reachable
- Vault Kubernetes auth works
- AVP can log in to Vault
- AVP can read `secret/data/mas/test-avp`
- AVP can replace `<path:...#key>` placeholders

Delete the test secret if desired:

```bash
oc exec -n vault vault-0 -- sh -c '
export VAULT_ADDR=http://127.0.0.1:8200
vault kv delete secret/mas/test-avp
'
```

## 14. Phase 12: Prepare IBM MAS repos

You need three GitLab repos:

```text
platform-gitops
mas-gitops-config
ibm-mas-gitops
```

### `platform-gitops`

Manages:

- Vault
- AVP
- ArgoCD setup
- IBM MAS Account Root app

### `ibm-mas-gitops`

Internal read-only mirror of IBM MAS GitOps source repo pinned to:

```text
8.0.0
```

### `mas-gitops-config`

Your MAS environment config repo.

Expected path:

```text
mas/drroc4/drgitopsapp/
```

## 15. Phase 13: Load real MAS secrets into Vault

Only after AVP test passes, load real MAS secrets.

PULL_B64=$(oc create secret docker-registry ibm-entitlement \
  --docker-server=cp.icr.io \
  --docker-username=cp \
  --docker-password="$IBM_ENTITLEMENT_KEY" \
  --dry-run=client -o jsonpath='{.data.\.dockerconfigjson}')

printf '%s' "$PULL_B64" > /tmp/pull.b64
oc cp /tmp/pull.b64 vault/vault-0:/tmp/pull.b64
vx "vault kv put secret/mas/drroc4/entitlement image_pull_secret_b64=@/tmp/pull.b64; rm -f /tmp/pull.b64"
rm -f /tmp/pull.b64

secrets to be cretaed are:
mas/drroc4/drgitopsapp/jdbc-system  ->  ca.crt
mas/drroc4/drgitopsapp/jdbc-system  ->  jdbc_url
mas/drroc4/drgitopsapp/jdbc-system  ->  password
mas/drroc4/drgitopsapp/jdbc-system  ->  username
mas/drroc4/drgitopsapp/license  ->  license_file
mas/drroc4/drgitopsapp/mongo  ->  ca.crt
mas/drroc4/drgitopsapp/mongo  ->  host
mas/drroc4/drgitopsapp/mongo  ->  password
mas/drroc4/drgitopsapp/mongo  ->  username
mas/drroc4/drgitopsapp/sls  ->  ca.crt
mas/drroc4/drgitopsapp/sls  ->  registration_key
mas/drroc4/drgitopsapp/sls  ->  url
mas/drroc4/drgitopsapp/sls-mongo  ->  ca.crt
mas/drroc4/drgitopsapp/sls-mongo  ->  password
mas/drroc4/drgitopsapp/sls-mongo  ->  username
mas/drroc4/entitlement  ->  image_pull_secret_b64

Expected paths:

```text
secret/data/mas/drroc4/entitlement

secret/data/mas/drroc4/drgitopsapp/license
secret/data/mas/drroc4/drgitopsapp/superuser
secret/data/mas/drroc4/drgitopsapp/mongo
secret/data/mas/drroc4/drgitopsapp/sls
secret/data/mas/drroc4/drgitopsapp/jdbc-system
secret/data/mas/drroc4/drgitopsapp/manage-crypto
```

Never commit real secret values to Git.

## 16. Phase 14: Prepare MAS config repo

In `mas-gitops-config`, confirm the generated structure:

```text
mas/drroc4/ibm-mas-cluster-base.yaml
mas/drroc4/ibm-operator-catalog.yaml
mas/drroc4/redhat-cert-manager.yaml
mas/drroc4/ibm-dro.yaml

mas/drroc4/drgitopsapp/ibm-mas-instance-base.yaml
mas/drroc4/drgitopsapp/ibm-sls.yaml
mas/drroc4/drgitopsapp/ibm-mas-suite.yaml
mas/drroc4/drgitopsapp/ibm-mas-suite-configs.yaml
mas/drroc4/drgitopsapp/ibm-mas-workspaces.yaml
mas/drroc4/drgitopsapp/ibm-mas-masapp-manage-install.yaml
mas/drroc4/drgitopsapp/ibm-mas-masapp-configs.yaml
```

Commit and push the config repo.

## 17. Phase 15: Register target cluster

From the management ArgoCD CLI context:

```bash
argocd cluster add <drroc4-context> --name drroc4
```

Verify:

```bash
argocd cluster list
```

Make sure the cluster URL in MAS config matches the registered cluster URL exactly.

## 18. Phase 16: Sync IBM MAS Account Root

Only sync MAS Account Root after all of these are complete:

- Vault running
- Vault unsealed
- Vault auth configured
- AVP sidecar working
- AVP test passed
- Real MAS secrets loaded into Vault
- MAS config repo committed
- Target cluster registered

Then sync:

```bash
argocd app sync ibm-mas-account-root
```

## 19. MAS deployment stages

### Stage 1: Cluster prerequisites

```text
ibm-mas-cluster-base.yaml
ibm-operator-catalog.yaml
redhat-cert-manager.yaml
ibm-dro.yaml
```

### Stage 2: MAS Core

```text
ibm-mas-instance-base.yaml
ibm-sls.yaml
ibm-mas-suite.yaml
ibm-mas-suite-configs.yaml
```

MAS Core requires MongoDB/SLS/license/entitlement, but it does not require the Manage application database.

### Stage 3: Workspace

```text
ibm-mas-workspaces.yaml
```

### Stage 4: Manage

```text
ibm-mas-masapp-manage-install.yaml
ibm-mas-masapp-configs.yaml
```

Do not deploy Manage until database/JDBC details are confirmed.

## 20. Troubleshooting

### GitLab x509 error

Error:

```text
x509: certificate signed by unknown authority
```

Fix:

```bash
oc apply -f bootstrap/00-gitlab-ca-configmap.yaml
oc rollout restart deploy/openshift-gitops-repo-server -n openshift-gitops
```

### GitLab authentication failed

Check:

- Exact deploy token username
- Token value
- `read_repository` scope
- Repo is under the same GitLab group prefix
- ArgoCD secret label is `argocd.argoproj.io/secret-type: repo-creds`

### Vault liveness probe restart loop

If Vault pods restart with:

```text
Liveness probe failed: HTTP probe failed with statuscode: 501
```

Vault is uninitialized. Do not initialize every pod. Initialize only `vault-0`, then join and unseal the others.

### Vault pod says not initialized

Only initialize `vault-0` once. Do not initialize `vault-1` or `vault-2` separately.

### AVP sidecar missing token

Error:

```text
open /var/run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount/token: no such file or directory
```

Use the working AVP patch with the projected service account token volume.

### AVP GLIBC error

Error:

```text
GLIBC_2.34 not found
```

Use UBI9 image for `avp-helm` sidecar.

### AVP cannot load backend configuration

Check Vault Kubernetes auth:

```bash
oc exec -n vault vault-0 -- sh -c '
export VAULT_ADDR=http://127.0.0.1:8200
vault read auth/kubernetes/config
vault read auth/kubernetes/role/mas-gitops
'
```

Also verify the repo-server service account has TokenReview permission.

## 21. Production improvement

The current working patch downloads AVP and Helm at repo-server startup.

For production, build or mirror an internal image that already contains:

```text
/usr/local/bin/argocd-vault-plugin
/usr/local/bin/helm
```

Then use:

```text
argocd/argocd-cr-avp-sidecar-patch-internal-image.example.yaml
```

This avoids runtime dependency on GitHub and `get.helm.sh`.

## 22. Cleanup

Delete apps:

```bash
oc delete application ibm-mas-account-root -n openshift-gitops --ignore-not-found
oc delete application hashicorp-vault-server -n openshift-gitops --ignore-not-found
oc delete application argocd-vault-plugin -n openshift-gitops --ignore-not-found
oc delete application platform-app-of-apps -n openshift-gitops --ignore-not-found
```

Delete AVP resources:

```bash
oc delete cm cmp-plugin -n openshift-gitops --ignore-not-found
oc delete secret argocd-vault-plugin-credentials -n openshift-gitops --ignore-not-found
```

Delete Vault only if you are okay deleting Vault data/PVCs:

```bash
oc delete project vault
```

## 23. Never commit these values

Never commit:

```text
Vault root token
Vault unseal keys
GitLab deploy token
IBM entitlement key
MAS license file content
MAS superuser password
Database password
Mongo password
SLS registration key
Manage crypto keys
Private keys
```

## File: gitlab/platform-gitops/values/values-management.yaml

account:
  id: mas

argoNamespace: openshift-gitops

# IMPORTANT: Use the actual repo-server service account name.
# In your current cluster this was observed as "default".
repoServerServiceAccount: default

source:
  repo_url: https://gitlab.lac1.biz/gitops/ibm-mas-gitops.git
  revision: "8.0.0"

generator:
  repo_url: https://gitlab.lac1.biz/gitops/mas-gitops-config.git
  revision: main

vault:
  enabled: true
  host: vault.apps.drroc4.lac1.biz
  chartVersion: "0.32.0"
  imageRepository: registry.connect.redhat.com/hashicorp/vault
  imageTag: "1.21.2-ubi"
  storageClass: isilon
  dataSize: 20Gi
  auditSize: 10Gi
  replicas: 3

avp:
  role: mas-gitops
  # Use in-cluster service URL to avoid external route TLS issues.
  vaultAddr: http://vault-active.vault.svc.cluster.local:8200

accountRoot:
  autoSync: false
  clusterAdminRole: true
  applicationAdminRole: true

## File: gitlab/platform-gitops/vault-auth/mas-gitops-policy.hcl

path "secret/data/mas/*" {
  capabilities = ["read", "list"]
}

path "secret/metadata/mas/*" {
  capabilities = ["read", "list"]
}

## File: gitlab/platform-gitops/vault-auth/setup-vault-auth.md

# Vault post-install setup for MAS GitOps

Vault initialization and unseal must remain manual/security-controlled.

## Manual steps

```bash
oc exec -n vault vault-0 -- vault operator init
oc exec -n vault vault-0 -- vault operator unseal '<KEY1>'
oc exec -n vault vault-0 -- vault operator unseal '<KEY2>'
oc exec -n vault vault-0 -- vault operator unseal '<KEY3>'
```

Join/unseal vault-1 and vault-2 if they are not joined:

```bash
oc exec -n vault vault-1 -- sh -c 'VAULT_ADDR=http://127.0.0.1:8200 vault operator raft join http://vault-0.vault-internal:8200'
oc exec -n vault vault-2 -- sh -c 'VAULT_ADDR=http://127.0.0.1:8200 vault operator raft join http://vault-0.vault-internal:8200'
```

Then run:

```bash
export VAULT_TOKEN='<root-or-admin-token>'
export REPO_SA=default
./vault-auth/setup-vault-auth.sh
```

## File: gitlab/platform-gitops/vault-auth/setup-vault-auth.sh

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
NAMESPACE_OPENSHIFT_GITOPS="${NAMESPACE_OPENSHIFT_GITOPS:-openshift-gitops}"
VAULT_NAMESPACE="${VAULT_NAMESPACE:-vault}"
VAULT_POD="${VAULT_POD:-vault-0}"
VAULT_ADDR_IN_POD="${VAULT_ADDR_IN_POD:-http://127.0.0.1:8200}"
VAULT_ROLE="${VAULT_ROLE:-mas-gitops}"
VAULT_POLICY="${VAULT_POLICY:-mas-gitops}"
REPO_SA="${REPO_SA:-default}"
if [[ -z "${VAULT_TOKEN:-}" ]]; then echo "ERROR: export VAULT_TOKEN first" >&2; exit 1; fi
K8S_HOST="$(oc whoami --show-server)"
TMPDIR="$(mktemp -d)"; trap 'rm -rf "$TMPDIR"' EXIT
oc create token "${REPO_SA}" -n "${NAMESPACE_OPENSHIFT_GITOPS}" --duration=24h > "${TMPDIR}/repo-server-token.txt"
oc get cm kube-root-ca.crt -n "${NAMESPACE_OPENSHIFT_GITOPS}" -o jsonpath='{.data.ca\.crt}' > "${TMPDIR}/kube-ca.crt"
oc cp "${TMPDIR}/repo-server-token.txt" "${VAULT_NAMESPACE}/${VAULT_POD}:/tmp/repo-server-token.txt"
oc cp "${TMPDIR}/kube-ca.crt" "${VAULT_NAMESPACE}/${VAULT_POD}:/tmp/kube-ca.crt"
oc exec -n "${VAULT_NAMESPACE}" "${VAULT_POD}" -- sh -c "export VAULT_ADDR=${VAULT_ADDR_IN_POD}; export VAULT_TOKEN='${VAULT_TOKEN}'; vault secrets enable -path=secret kv-v2 || true"
oc cp "$(dirname "$0")/mas-gitops-policy.hcl" "${VAULT_NAMESPACE}/${VAULT_POD}:/tmp/mas-gitops-policy.hcl"
oc exec -n "${VAULT_NAMESPACE}" "${VAULT_POD}" -- sh -c "export VAULT_ADDR=${VAULT_ADDR_IN_POD}; export VAULT_TOKEN='${VAULT_TOKEN}'; vault policy write ${VAULT_POLICY} /tmp/mas-gitops-policy.hcl"
oc exec -n "${VAULT_NAMESPACE}" "${VAULT_POD}" -- sh -c "export VAULT_ADDR=${VAULT_ADDR_IN_POD}; export VAULT_TOKEN='${VAULT_TOKEN}'; vault auth enable kubernetes || true"
oc exec -n "${VAULT_NAMESPACE}" "${VAULT_POD}" -- sh -c "export VAULT_ADDR=${VAULT_ADDR_IN_POD}; export VAULT_TOKEN='${VAULT_TOKEN}'; vault write auth/kubernetes/config token_reviewer_jwt=\"\$(cat /tmp/repo-server-token.txt)\" kubernetes_host='${K8S_HOST}' kubernetes_ca_cert=@/tmp/kube-ca.crt"
oc exec -n "${VAULT_NAMESPACE}" "${VAULT_POD}" -- sh -c "export VAULT_ADDR=${VAULT_ADDR_IN_POD}; export VAULT_TOKEN='${VAULT_TOKEN}'; vault write auth/kubernetes/role/${VAULT_ROLE} bound_service_account_names='${REPO_SA}' bound_service_account_namespaces='${NAMESPACE_OPENSHIFT_GITOPS}' policies='${VAULT_POLICY}' ttl=1h"
oc adm policy add-cluster-role-to-user system:auth-delegator "system:serviceaccount:${NAMESPACE_OPENSHIFT_GITOPS}:${REPO_SA}" || true
oc exec -n "${VAULT_NAMESPACE}" "${VAULT_POD}" -- sh -c "export VAULT_ADDR=${VAULT_ADDR_IN_POD}; export VAULT_TOKEN='${VAULT_TOKEN}'; vault read auth/kubernetes/role/${VAULT_ROLE}"

## File: gitlab/platform-gitops/vault-auth/test-avp.sh

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
VAULT_NAMESPACE="${VAULT_NAMESPACE:-vault}"
VAULT_POD="${VAULT_POD:-vault-0}"
ARGO_NAMESPACE="${ARGO_NAMESPACE:-openshift-gitops}"
VAULT_ADDR_IN_POD="${VAULT_ADDR_IN_POD:-http://127.0.0.1:8200}"
VAULT_ADDR_FOR_AVP="${VAULT_ADDR_FOR_AVP:-http://vault-active.vault.svc.cluster.local:8200}"
AVP_ROLE="${AVP_ROLE:-mas-gitops}"
if [[ -z "${VAULT_TOKEN:-}" ]]; then echo "ERROR: export VAULT_TOKEN first" >&2; exit 1; fi
oc exec -n "${VAULT_NAMESPACE}" "${VAULT_POD}" -- sh -c "export VAULT_ADDR=${VAULT_ADDR_IN_POD}; export VAULT_TOKEN='${VAULT_TOKEN}'; vault kv put secret/mas/test-avp message='hello-from-vault'"
POD="$(oc get pod -n "${ARGO_NAMESPACE}" | awk '/repo-server/ {print $1; exit}')"
oc exec -n "${ARGO_NAMESPACE}" "${POD}" -c avp-helm -- sh -c "
export AVP_TYPE=vault
export AVP_AUTH_TYPE=k8s
export AVP_K8S_ROLE=${AVP_ROLE}
export VAULT_ADDR=${VAULT_ADDR_FOR_AVP}
cat <<'EOF' | argocd-vault-plugin generate -
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: avp-test
  namespace: openshift-gitops
data:
  message: \"<path:secret/data/mas/test-avp#message>\"
EOF
"
